verses.2: “If someone ventures to talk with you, will you be grieved?
verses.4: Your words have supported him who was falling,
verses.5: But now it has come to you, and you faint.
verses.7: “Remember, now, who ever perished, being innocent?
verses.8: According to what I have seen, those who plow iniquity
verses.9: By the breath of God they perish.
verses.11: The old lion perishes for lack of prey.
verses.12: “Now a thing was secretly brought to me.
verses.13: In thoughts from the visions of the night,
verses.14: fear came on me, and trembling,
verses.15: Then a spirit passed before my face.
verses.16: It stood still, but I couldn’t discern its appearance.
verses.17: ‘Shall mortal man be more just than God?
verses.18: Behold, he puts no trust in his servants.
verses.19: How much more those who dwell in houses of clay,
verses.20: Between morning and evening they are destroyed.
verses.21: Isn’t their tent cord plucked up within them?
